tools.name: politics_represent
tools.description: Find who represents a US ZIP code: both U.S. Senators, the U.S. House member for that ZIP's congressional district, and the state's statewide executives (governor, attorney general, etc.). Every official includes source_url (the capture origin) and source_link (a public page you can open). Use this

tools.name: politics_search
tools.description: Search for politicians and elected officials across all levels of US government — federal, state, county, municipal. Filter by name, state, party, level, chamber, or office title. Returns officials with their current office, party, and contact info.

tools.name: politics_official
tools.description: Get full details for a specific official by ID — biography, party, office, policy positions, committee assignments, campaign finance, social media, and contact info. All data includes source URLs for verification.

tools.name: politics_by_state
tools.description: Get all current officials for a US state — federal senators and representatives, state legislators, and any indexed local officials. Organized by government level.

tools.name: politics_positions
tools.description: Search policy positions across all officials by topic. Find who supports or opposes specific issues (crypto, stablecoins, AI, healthcare, guns, etc.). Each position includes source URL + origin hash for verification. Returns COMPACT records by default (id, name, party, state, stance, summary, source

tools.name: politics_stats
tools.description: Get summary statistics for the politics.rootz.global database — total officials, breakdown by level, party, state, and chamber. Shows coverage completeness.

tools.name: politics_timeline
tools.description: Get the full origin timeline for a politician — every page snapshot, every position change, every data source with hash proof. The political wayback machine. Shows what changed, when, and where the data came from.

tools.name: politics_changes
tools.description: Get recent changes detected across all politicians — position shifts, website updates, new data. Each change links to the before/after snapshots with origin proof.

tools.name: politics_origin
tools.description: Get origin archive statistics — total snapshots, changes detected, origin proofs recorded, unique URLs tracked. Shows the depth of the political wayback machine.

tools.name: politics_bridge_create
tools.description: Create a citizen bridge page — a personalized workspace for tracking representatives, comparing positions, and taking action (write letters, log calls). Returns a shareable URL that any AI agent can collaborate on. Optionally auto-populates with the citizen's state representatives.

tools.name: politics_bridge_session
tools.description: Get the current state of a citizen bridge page — tracked officials, comparisons, drafted actions, and messages. Use this to understand what the citizen is working on.

tools.name: politics_bridge_compare
tools.description: Compare multiple politicians side-by-side on a bridge page — positions, party, office, campaign finance. Optionally filter to a specific topic.

tools.name: politics_bridge_letter
tools.description: Draft a letter to a politician on the citizen's bridge page. The letter gets an origin hash for proof of content. Returns delivery options (email, web form, USPS, phone).

tools.name: politics_bill
tools.description: Get details of a US bill or resolution — vote breakdown, sponsors, cosponsors, status, and origin proof. By default returns metadata + a short text excerpt + a text index (full_text omitted, since omnibus bills exceed the token cap). For the actual legislative language, use politics_bill_toc (sectio

tools.name: politics_bill_toc
tools.description: Get the section index (table of contents) for a bill: every "SEC. N" heading with its character offset and length, plus the whole-bill origin hash (content_hash) and total text length. Small response. Use this to see a large bill's structure, then fetch specific sections with politics_bill_text.

tools.name: politics_bill_text
tools.description: Get one signed, bounded slice of a bill's full legislative text, by part number or section label. Response carries part/total_parts, next_part, this slice's part_sha256, and the whole-bill content_hash. Provenance: fetch parts 1..total_parts in order, concatenate their text, SHA-256 -> equals conten

tools.name: politics_bills_search
tools.description: Search bills by topic, sponsor, status, or congress. Returns bill metadata and whether full text is available. Use /api/topic/:key/bills for topic-specific legislation.

tools.name: politics_candidate_feed
tools.description: Get a candidate's Campaign Data Standard (CDS) feed: a single machine-readable record of who they are, the offices they hold and seek, their positions in their own words (each source-linked), their voting record by reference, and declared data gaps. This is the standardized, signed feed an AI or rep

tools.name: politics_methodology
tools.description: Get the full transparency methodology — how we collect, assess, extract, sign, and verify political data. Includes quality assessment algorithm (signals + weights), trust levels, data sources, cron schedule, and what we can and cannot prove. Open and auditable.

tools.name: politics_provenance
tools.description: Get the complete origin proof chain for an official — every data source, content hash, quality assessment, snapshot history, and verification instructions. Proves where every piece of data came from.

tools.name: politics_speeches
tools.description: Search Congressional Record floor speeches by official, topic, date range, or keyword. Returns verbatim text from the official government record with origin proof hash. Every speech is directly citable with a GPO source URL.

tools.name: politics_candidates
tools.description: List registered federal candidates for the 2026 election cycle. Filter by state, office (senate/house/president), party, or whether they have a campaign URL. Returns campaign website, social media links, FEC data, and position/speech counts. Use this to discover who is running for office in any stat

tools.name: politics_state_votes
tools.description: Search state legislature votes across 26+ US states. Filter by state, topic, or date. Returns bill title, vote result, yes/no counts.

tools.name: politics_municipalities
tools.description: Search US municipalities (cities, towns, villages, counties). Every incorporated place in the US with coordinates. Filter by state, name, or type. Returns website, platform, meeting calendar, and board/commission info where available.

tools.name: politics_create_watchlist
tools.description: Create a Just the Facts watchlist for a user so an AI can construct it on their behalf. Provide email plus any of: officials (array of IDs from politics_search/politics_by_state), topics (array of issue names), or a town (+state) to track local meetings, agendas, and minutes for that town. Sends a d

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: politics_official, politics_positions, politics_origin, politics_bridge_session, politics_candidate_feed, politics_speeches, politics_candidates, politics_create_watchlist · Private data: politics_search, politics_official, politics_positions, politics_stats, politics_bill, politics_bill_text, politics_candidate_feed, politics_speeches · Public sink: politics_bridge_letter, politics_create_watchlist.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: politics_official, politics_positions, politics_origin, politics_bridge_session, politics_candidate_feed, politics_speeches, politics_candidates, politics_create_watchlist · Private data: politics_search, politics_official, politics_positions, politics_stats, politics_bill, politics_bill_text, politics_candidate_feed, politics_speeches · Public sink: politics_bridge_letter, politics_create_watchlist

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
